title: Scope decision: timer dashboard empty state in inline-entity-creation work unit
decision: Option C — tactical bounce-link fix in scope; true inline-create on the dashboard deferred to follow-up work unit.

context.problem: src/tech/jgood/gleanmo/timer/routes.clj:343-344 renders bare text ('No <parent>s found. Create some first!') with no button, link, or escape hatch when a user has zero parent entities (projects/books/meditations). Users landing on a timer dashboard with no parents are stuck. The inline-entity-creation work unit could address this, but it raises a scope question: the work unit's stated boundary is 'form-only', and the dashboard is a list-view surface.
constraints: Work unit should stay bounded to land in reasonable time.
The dashboard dead-end is a real UX bug that should not wait indefinitely.
True inline-create on a list-view surface is a different shape from form-level inline-create (no parent <select> to swap back into).

options.label: Defer entirely
options.description: Leave the dead-end as-is in this work unit. Track as a follow-up work unit only. Don't touch timer/routes.clj.

options.label: Scope-creep: real inline-create on dashboard
options.description: Implement true inline-create on the timer dashboard parent-picker: render a select + mini-form inline on the dashboard itself. Requires infrastructure for the list-view surface (no parent <form> to ride on).

options.label: Tactical bounce-link now, real inline later
options.description: Two-line tactical fix: replace the bare text with a bounce-link to the parent new-form with redirect back (mirrors workout.clj:355-357). True inline-create on the dashboard tracked as follow-up work unit timer-dashboard-inline-create.md.

criteria.label: Minimizes rework when real inline lands

matrix.notes: Dead-end stays; bug ships to prod indefinitely.
Real solution but blows scope; list-view infrastructure is a different shape.
Mirrors workout.clj:355-357 already shipped; tactical link is removed when real inline lands.

rationale: Option C removes the dead-end immediately at trivial cost, mirrors an existing pattern (workout picker), and keeps the work unit bounded. The bounce-link will be removed when the follow-up work unit ships real inline-create on the dashboard surface. Option A leaves a known UX bug in production indefinitely. Option B is the right long-term answer but requires infrastructure (list-view inline-create) that does not exist yet and would unbalance this work unit.
follow_ups: Workout picker at app/workout.clj:352-358 will also be replaced by Phase 2 of inline-entity-creation.md; same shape of tactical-vs-real trade-off resolved the same way.
